title: Adiabatic Processes and Heat Capacity Measurement
content: In an adiabatic process no heat is exchanged with surroundings (Q = 0). Adding a known electrical energy Q = V·I·t to an insulated sample of mass m raises its temperature by ΔT, giving Cp = Q/(m·ΔT) in J/(g·K). Practical adiabatic calorimeters use Dewar flasks and guard heaters to minimise parasitic losses. Correction for heat capacity of the vessel (Cv_vessel) is applied: Q = (m·Cp + Cv_vessel)·ΔT.
